text: Professor: Alan Fowler Review: This guy is a slow lecturer and it may be beneficial to listen to the recordings instead of going to class. His lectures are very boring and the material can feel uninteresting. Review: The professor can be harsh toward students who ask questions he considers obvious or simple. Some students find this intimidating, while others find it entertaining. Review: Pro: Allo

r simple. Some students find this intimidating, while others find it entertaining. Review: Pro: Allows group homework for assignments. Students with +2 years of computer background did well. Cons: I could not bear him teaching us to solve simple questions for 40 minutes. I only attended the lectures because I wanted to complete the group assignment with friends. I wish I had taken TCSS 371 with Pr

because I wanted to complete the group assignment with friends. I wish I had taken TCSS 371 with Professor Manaka in the Summer. Review: Course was organized fairly well. Don't expect to get grades back on any assignments until you're about to take the final. Heavily geared towards assessments - beware of trick questions on early quizzes. Tends to get frustrated with trivial questions which won't

ware of trick questions on early quizzes. Tends to get frustrated with trivial questions which won't work for new students. Very good for high-level courses; all others, stay away. Review: He is very thorough with his lectures and I really learned the content. His assignments aren't too hard and he allows you to partners. He gives extra credit opportunities,1 quiz score is dropped, and the final i

allows you to partners. He gives extra credit opportunities,1 quiz score is dropped, and the final is worth only 10%. You are allowed to 1 late assignment without penalty. As long as you show up and try you should do fine in the class. Review: I've had Fowler for all my CS classes so far at UWT, and he's pretty solid. He's a straight shooter and says it how it is. 342 was solid. I finished the cla

he's pretty solid. He's a straight shooter and says it how it is. 342 was solid. I finished the class feeling ready for what's next. My CS knowledge has grown a lot thanks to Fowler. Solid dude. The lecturing can be a little slow, but he doesn't try to overwhelm you with too much. Review: He's cool for most basic stuff, but he does not go into depth on anything. Ask questions? He'll reiterate exa

for most basic stuff, but he does not go into depth on anything. Ask questions? He'll reiterate exactly what he just said and not help you actually understand, so you better hope you get it the first time. Not to mention, he's insanely boring. I wouldn't sleep through your class if your lectures were interesting professor. Review: Super solid professor, asked a few classmates after the quarter an

re interesting professor. Review: Super solid professor, asked a few classmates after the quarter and most were content with him. Lectures showcase how to start homework and with in-class examples relating to it. I really like how he shows multiple ways to do something. Do beware of the weekly quizzes though, they're short but can hurt! Super chill professor to take. Review: He responded rudely to

es though, they're short but can hurt! Super chill professor to take. Review: He responded rudely to asking questions in class instead of asking us to wait until the lecture is over. Then during break told us not to ask specific questions in class and gives vague instructions about the content.

Professor: Charles Bryan Review: He has a good sense of humor and keeps the class engaging, even if his jokes didn't always land for me. The class was enjoyable overall. His exams were tough at first, but once he started giving study guides, they became much more manageable. Review: Its clear this professor is passionate about coding, his lectures are fun and engaging, he's a lenient grader and bu

rofessor is passionate about coding, his lectures are fun and engaging, he's a lenient grader and bulk of your grade will be from assigned projects, there is a midterm/final 'quiz' but its not heavily graded so if you don't do well coding on paper; don't worry. There is a quarter long project you will be working on w/ assigned team Review: Lectures always very useful, Prof. is great at answering q

ll be working on w/ assigned team Review: Lectures always very useful, Prof. is great at answering questions. Lectures are recorded so you won't miss anything (except the opportunity to ask clarifying questions). Attendance is only mandatory on quiz days. Quizzes are on paper (written code) and material is provided around a week in advance. Group project at the end, not too difficult. Review: Char

rial is provided around a week in advance. Group project at the end, not too difficult. Review: Charles is the most enthusiastic professor I've had at this school. I already enjoy CS but his passion for teaching and CS is contagious. Super available for help. He's smart and wants you to succeed. Give this man a raise, carrying the whole cs department with one (maybe two) other professors. Review:

e this man a raise, carrying the whole cs department with one (maybe two) other professors. Review: Spring 2022 - Quarter long project with labs. Lectures are funny, informative, and exceptionally detailed. Project is extremely fascinating and fun, but communicate with the professor if there are any issues with your group. Labs are 100% essential - pay close attention and take notes. Extremely hea

issues with your group. Labs are 100% essential - pay close attention and take notes. Extremely heavy course, but worth every second of your time. Review: I appreciate him for teaching Java Swing framework. I am little biased for comparing his teaching materials to UW Allen school materials. If you want to maintain coding skills, don't forget to practice LeetCode questions concurrently. Expect to
want to maintain coding skills, don't forget to practice LeetCode questions concurrently. Expect to spend hours reading Java Swing documentation.

Professor: David Schuessler Review: Professor Schuessler is genuinely one of the kindest and most helpful instructors I've had in a long time. The course material was challenging, but his patience and willingness to explain concepts thoroughly made everything easier. His office hours were incredibly useful, he never made anyone feel rushed or uncomfortable for needing extra help. Review: David is

useful, he never made anyone feel rushed or uncomfortable for needing extra help. Review: David is indeed a tough grader, but this comes from a care about his students learning the proper techniques and being consistent with the material. Also David is more than happy to help people who are stuck on assignments or have questions. Overall, David is set on making sure his students enter the compute

n assignments or have questions. Overall, David is set on making sure his students enter the computer science major as ready as possible. Review: His knowledge and experience is all academic and extremely dated. He insists on conventions invented by him and not used by anyone in the professional world. His quizzes are on extremely short timers making them more of a test of speed than ability. He m

ld. His quizzes are on extremely short timers making them more of a test of speed than ability. He might be the worst professor I have had yet. I would never take another class with him. Review: Schuessler will be many students first experience with Java, and he often misses the mark when attempting to connect to those struggling. He teaches on the whiteboard without interactive exercise, leading

to connect to those struggling. He teaches on the whiteboard without interactive exercise, leading to a lack of comprehension as the class watches him write. His attitude is not conductive to maintaining class attention or morale. Review: This class is designed to separate the weak from the strong. It is NOT meant to make you a better programmer. It is meant to make you question if you have the w

It is NOT meant to make you a better programmer. It is meant to make you question if you have the will-power to move forward, which is not necessarily a bad thing for aspiring programmers. I think this also shows in Schuesslers teaching and why a lot of students are not a fan. Review: The lectures don't fully explain how or why things happen and the prof is condescending towards students leaving

don't fully explain how or why things happen and the prof is condescending towards students leaving them afraid to ask questions. Lectures are 95% powerpoint and coding examples are solutions already written by the prof. The class needs more thorough explanation of concepts and examples from scratch. Unwelcoming class, probably avoid.
. Unwelcoming class, probably avoid.

Professor: Jeffery Weiss Review: This professor is by far one of the worst professors I have ever taken at UW Tacoma. His lectures are super dry and do not expect to learn anything from them. The course is centered around a group project and he doesn't allow students to choose their own partners. Canvas is never updated with when projects/assignments are due and is unorganized. Review: Hmm... how

nvas is never updated with when projects/assignments are due and is unorganized. Review: Hmm... how did someone become a professor for a cs program without a cs degree? That should tell you everything. I dont think he knows how to code. Review: The course revolves around a group project. The project was well organized and helpful for understanding the course concepts. However, some of the expectat

was well organized and helpful for understanding the course concepts. However, some of the expectations for the project changed which was frustrating. Lectures were decent, but a little bit slow. Review: This class felt like a complete waste of time, and I barely learned anything. Topics that should have taken 10 minutes to be taught is dragged out over 2 hours. Furthermore, the presentation of l

d have taken 10 minutes to be taught is dragged out over 2 hours. Furthermore, the presentation of lectures is very unorganized as the lecturer comes in without a plan (he even told us so). A single group project is the main focus of the class. No tests. Review: Does not provide practical knowledge or useful information. Simply refers us to other sites to find information. Covers main concepts rel

or useful information. Simply refers us to other sites to find information. Covers main concepts related to the design of software, but his lectures are useless. Expect to learn on your own time. Half the class is dedicated to brainstorming? This class was a total joke.

UW Tacoma Professor Discussion Student: Charles Bryan is often recommended because of his enthusiasm and willingness to help students succeed. Student: Alan Fowler is known for being thorough and structured. Some students appreciate his organization while others find the lectures slow-paced. Student: David Schuessler is considered a challenging instructor. Students frequently mention high standard

: David Schuessler is considered a challenging instructor. Students frequently mention high standards and rigorous grading. Student: Tom Capaul receives praise for project-based learning and connecting classroom concepts to industry experience. Student: Many students recommend researching professors before registration because teaching styles vary significantly. Student: The best professor for one

before registration because teaching styles vary significantly. Student: The best professor for one student may not be the best for another. Learning preferences and previous experience often influence opinions. Student: Students who attend lectures regularly generally report better outcomes than students who rely entirely on recordings.
tudents who rely entirely on recordings.

UW Tacoma Computer Science Discussion Student: I transferred into the CSS program and found the class sizes much smaller than Seattle. It was easier to get help from professors and build relationships with classmates. Student: Many of the upper-level classes rely heavily on group projects. Time management and communication are important because several courses have quarter-long projects. Student:

gement and communication are important because several courses have quarter-long projects. Student: The program is challenging but manageable if you stay on top of assignments. Students who procrastinate often struggle because deadlines can pile up quickly. Student: Office hours are one of the best resources available. Most professors are willing to help students who make an effort and ask questio

resources available. Most professors are willing to help students who make an effort and ask questions. Student: Internships and personal projects are important. Several students mentioned that coursework alone was not enough preparation for technical interviews. Student: Software Engineering and Database courses are commonly recommended because they involve practical projects that can be discusse

tabase courses are commonly recommended because they involve practical projects that can be discussed during interviews. Student: Some courses are theory-heavy while others focus on implementation. Students often recommend balancing difficult courses with lighter electives.

Professor: Tom Capaul Review: One of the best of the CS department. Knows his stuff, works very well with students, encourages AI use for learning. The class is as hard as your team makes it, and Tom will let you know so. He gives excellent project feedback and is clear what he expects from his students. This class lets you show what you know and Tom will help push you further. Review: An amazing

ents. This class lets you show what you know and Tom will help push you further. Review: An amazing professor, anyone rating this angel of a man badly is trying to save a spot in his classes for themselves. He is amazing, caring and makes you actually want to learn, not just rush to turn things in on time. He is one of the best lecturers on the campus!! Review: Great professor, very supportive and

n time. He is one of the best lecturers on the campus!! Review: Great professor, very supportive and not the issue here. Class is project-based (no exams/hw, yes for quizzes) so success depends heavily on group work. Be very careful who you partner with, my group made the course emotionally draining despite the projects not being hard. Loved the professor, but the group experience was discouraging

despite the projects not being hard. Loved the professor, but the group experience was discouraging & depressing. Review: I took TCSS305, not 360 (option wasn't available to select). Tom is easily one of the best professors at UWT for the CS program. He records lectures, has a very clear grading policy, walks through new concepts confidently, and is an overall cool dude. Wish I could've talked to

licy, walks through new concepts confidently, and is an overall cool dude. Wish I could've talked to him more, but hopefully in spring. Review: Tom is easily one of the best professors at UW Tacoma. He genuinely cares about his students and has a wealth of academic and industry knowledge. 360 is essentially one big group project, with a lot of freedom to explore new material if you desire. I highl

entially one big group project, with a lot of freedom to explore new material if you desire. I highly recommend him for 305 and 483 as well. If you put the work in, you'll get an A. Review: This professor knows his stuff. He experienced the real world and he wants to give that back to you with a little bit of cushion. To get the most out of this class, I recommend meeting with him often to discuss

tle bit of cushion. To get the most out of this class, I recommend meeting with him often to discuss code, group stuff, project direction, etc. If code has yet to click with you, this may be the class you've been waiting for. Review: Tom is so passionate, and so in it for the students. He sets a great example of selflessness for us. It is, in fact, safe to say that I got a little bit of faith in h

at example of selflessness for us. It is, in fact, safe to say that I got a little bit of faith in humanity back after my quarter with Tom. He challenges you, but he's with you all the way. I only wish I'd found more opportunities to study under him.

UW Tacoma Computer Science and Systems Program The Computer Science and Systems program prepares students for careers in software development, information technology, cybersecurity, data science, and related fields. Students complete coursework in programming, data structures, algorithms, software engineering, operating systems, databases, networking, and computer architecture. Many courses includ

ngineering, operating systems, databases, networking, and computer architecture. Many courses include group projects that simulate real-world software development environments. Students gain experience working with programming languages, development tools, testing techniques, and collaborative workflows. The curriculum encourages critical thinking, problem solving, and communication skills. Gradua

lows. The curriculum encourages critical thinking, problem solving, and communication skills. Graduates pursue careers in software engineering, systems administration, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and technology consulting. Internships, personal projects, and extracurricular learning are commonly recommended to strengthen career readiness.
recommended to strengthen career readiness.

University of Washington Tacoma School of Engineering and Technology Computer Science and Systems faculty teach courses related to programming, software engineering, databases, algorithms, networking, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and data science. Faculty members support student learning through lectures, office hours, research opportunities, and project supervision. The department emph

through lectures, office hours, research opportunities, and project supervision. The department emphasizes practical experience through team projects, software development practices, and industry-relevant technologies. Students are encouraged to meet with faculty members to discuss coursework, internships, research opportunities, and career planning. The program combines theoretical foundations wi
nships, research opportunities, and career planning. The program combines theoretical foundations with hands-on technical skills.

Professor: Wes Lloyd Review: had this professor as of spring 2024,was good not bad, sometimes he wasn't as clear on explaining thing. He was way too strict about academic dishonesty went off on student that was just asking for help on setting up the software that was needed . 4 months after the course, accused 8 students of cheating with no hard evidence, including me. Review: Professor Llyod is o

se, accused 8 students of cheating with no hard evidence, including me. Review: Professor Llyod is one of the best professors I've studied under. One can see that he is really passionate about teaching. He stays back and answers every student's questions, sometimes even going to the extent of going over their code and debugging it. Assignments are interesting and the grading is easy. Definitely ta

over their code and debugging it. Assignments are interesting and the grading is easy. Definitely take a course under him. Review: One of the few professors who really put an effort into making sure that he was accessible if we had any questions. He made extra class sessions to review pre-exam practice questions, and also had experienced Software Engineers come to class and tell us their stories.

ice questions, and also had experienced Software Engineers come to class and tell us their stories. I believe he one of the best Professors at UW Tacoma. Review: Wes is the most patient professor I have ever come across. During the quarter, he gained a reputation of being super helpful during office hours, even if you caught him after class. He would take the time to explain clearly and has a keen

hours, even if you caught him after class. He would take the time to explain clearly and has a keen ability to understand exactly what you are having trouble with. Not an easy class, but a worthwhile one! Review: Good lecturer. Uses a free online textbook. Provides grading rubrics for each assignment up-front, and each assignment has bonus extra credit options. Always willing to answer questions.

nt up-front, and each assignment has bonus extra credit options. Always willing to answer questions. Programming assignments focus on multithreading and parallel processing, using C. Review: I've had lots of professors and Dr. Lloyd is without a doubt at the top of the list. Lectures are boring but otherwise he is extremely helpful, knows a lot, and very fair on grading. Practice tests that were v

otherwise he is extremely helpful, knows a lot, and very fair on grading. Practice tests that were very similar to what was on the actual tests. His tutorials are very helpful for homework. Lectures, slides, and book all follow each other. Review: Great professor and a great class. The class was challenging, and you will learn a lot. The textbook he uses is free and you'll definitely need to read

llenging, and you will learn a lot. The textbook he uses is free and you'll definitely need to read it if you want to do well on the exams, which are pretty tough. His homework is time-consuming, but if you know C well, you'll do fine. Make sure you do the extra credit offered. Review: The best kind of professor. Wants you to learn the material, not just regurgitate from a book. Will explain it to

of professor. Wants you to learn the material, not just regurgitate from a book. Will explain it to you as many times as needed, and in detail. Available after class and during office hours. Genuinely cares about producing the best of the next gen of computer scientists. The work is difficult, but satisfying. Great guy.
satisfying. Great guy.
